doc_title: Smurfs: Leveraging Multiple Proficiency Agents with Context-Efficiency for Tool Planning

items.evidence_span: For example, the search engine API empowers ChatGPT to access real-time information (Zhao et al., 2023).
These challenges include computational expense and a lack of adaptability to new tools (Hao et al., 2024; Guu et al., 2020; Qin et al., 2024).
These challenges include computational expense and a lack of adaptability to new tools (Hao et al., 2024; Guu et al., 2020; Qin et al., 2024).
These challenges include computational expense and a lack of adaptability to new tools (Hao et al., 2024; Guu et al., 2020; Qin et al., 2024).

This paper addresses the critical research problem of enhancing the problem-solving capabilities of LLMs through the adoption of a multi-agent system (MAS) framework (Dorri et al., 2018; Van der Hoek and Wooldridge, 2008).

items.fact: Smurfs cites Van der Hoek and Wooldridge (2008)
items.evidence_span: This paper addresses the critical research problem of enhancing the problem-solving capabilities of LLMs through the adoption of a multi-agent system (MAS) framework (Dorri et al., 2018; Van der Hoek and Wooldridge, 2008).
(Qin et al., 2024) introduces ToolBench, an instruction-tuning dataset for tool usage
Another significant contribution is Gorilla (Patil et al., 2023), which excels at writing API calls

items.fact: Smurfs utilizes the mistral7b-instruct model (Jiang et al., 2023)
items.evidence_span: utilizing the mistral7b-instruct model (Jiang et al., 2023)

items.fact: Smurfs is evaluated against the benchmark set by ChatGPT-ReACT (Yao et al., 2022)
items.evidence_span: against the benchmark set by ChatGPT-ReACT (Yao et al., 2022)

items.fact: Evaluation was done on the ToolBench I2 and I3 benchmark (Qin et al., 2024)
items.evidence_span: on the ToolBench I2 and I3 benchmark (Qin et al., 2024)
(Liu et al., 2024)
(Petroni et al., 2020)
(Ram et al., 2023)
(Mallen et al., 2022)
(Dai et al., 2019)
(Dao et al., 2022)
(Shi et al., 2023)
(Du et al., 2023)
(Liang et al., 2023)

items.fact: Executor Agent uses an external tool library (Qin et al., 2024)
items.evidence_span: The Executor Agent is responsible for choosing and executing the tools to solve the sub-tasks. The agent has access to an external tool library (Qin et al., 2024).

items.fact: Answer Agent uses the ReACT format (Yao et al., 2022)
items.evidence_span: the agent, using the ReACT format (Yao et al., 2022) to choose the tool and arguments, then execute the tool.

items.fact: Verifier Agent uses a depth-first search with an early stopping strategy inspired by (Qin et al., 2024)
items.evidence_span: inspired by (Qin et al., 2024), we employ a depth-first search with an early stopping strategy to find the solution path.
